name: Acrostic suicide note (Rubaiyat-themed)
description: The cipher encodes the first letters of words in a self-composed farewell verse in the style of the Rubaiyat, written by Carl Webb as a suicide note.
supporting_evidence: Crossed-out line (draft correction pattern)
MLIA prefix repeat (same phrase, two attempts)
IC = 0.074 matches English initial-letter distribution
Chi-squared passes acrostic test
4 content lines match Rubaiyat stanza structure
Tamam Shud slip = 'It is finished'
Carl Webb: poetry lover, mental decline
Digitalis/ouabain poisoning (self-administered)
Entropy and vowel ratio consistent with English initials

name: Acrostic love letter / farewell to woman
description: The cipher is an acrostic love letter or farewell to Jessica Thomson, composed on the book she was connected to.
supporting_evidence: Jessica Thomson's phone number on same book cover
She went pale when shown the plaster death mask
MLIA could be 'My Love Is A...'
Body found near her home
All evidence for H1 also applies
contradicting_evidence: She denied knowing the man
Suicide-specific evidence (poison, Tamam Shud) slightly favors H1 over H2
note: H1 and H2 are not mutually exclusive; the most likely truth is a farewell to a lover combined with a suicide note.

name: Acrostic personal memo (mundane)
description: The cipher is a mundane personal note (horse racing tips, to-do list, etc.) written as an initial-letter abbreviation.
supporting_evidence: IC and chi-squared consistent with acrostic
Webb was a horse gambler
contradicting_evidence: Written on a poetry book (not a racing form)
Tamam Shud slip has no mundane explanation
Suicide by poison doesn't fit mundane context

name: Espionage one-time pad
description: The cipher is an encrypted spy message using a one-time pad or similar cryptographic system.
supporting_evidence: Cold War timing (1948)
Removed clothing labels (spy tradecraft)
Jessica Thomson had communist connections
contradicting_evidence: FATAL: Crossed-out line impossible in OTP
FATAL: MLIA prefix repeat impossible with OTP key reuse
IC = 0.074 far from random 0.038 expected for OTP
Entropy too low for encrypted output
Carl Webb not a known intelligence operative
DNA identification fits civilian profile
elimination_reason: The crossed-out line with shared MLIA prefix is structurally impossible in any one-time pad system. This single observation reduces OTP probability to effectively zero.

name: Espionage book cipher (Rubaiyat as key)
description: The letters encode positions or word indices in the Rubaiyat, used as a book cipher key.
supporting_evidence: The Rubaiyat was present (book cipher needs the book)
Spy book ciphers were used in the Cold War era
contradicting_evidence: No Rubaiyat stanza matches any cipher line above 50%
Crossed-out line inconsistent with book cipher extraction
Webb identified as civilian, not spy

name: Random / meaningless doodle
description: The letters are meaningless scribbles with no encoded content.
contradicting_evidence: IC far from random (0.074 vs 0.038)
MLIA repeat extremely unlikely by chance (P ~ 1/14000)
Entropy too structured for random
Line structure implies intentional segmentation
Connected to Tamam Shud slip and death
Webb was a known poetry enthusiast
